haiku_classifier._doc: Optional Haiku-based intent classifier. When enabled, the hook falls back to a Haiku call if keyword matching produces no hit. Haiku reads the user prompt + persona catalog and returns a structured suggestion. Adds ~1–2s latency per non-keyword-matching prompt and ~$0.001 quota each. Set enabled=true to opt in. The keyword path always runs first (cheap and deterministic); Haiku is the no-keyword fallback only.

rules.phrasing: High-stakes document prompt detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-adversarial-document-reviewer` for adversarial premise-challenging, assumption-surfacing, and decision stress-testing on architecture and requirements docs.

High-risk diff or adversarial-review framing. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-adversarial-reviewer` for chaos-engineer review that constructs specific failure chains across auth, payments, data migrations, and external APIs.

Prompt looks agent-parity-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-agent-native-reviewer` for a focused audit of action/context parity between UI features and agent tools, including system-prompt completeness.
Gem README authoring detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-ankane-readme-writer` for Ankane-template READMEs with imperative voice, standard section ordering, and Ruby gem conventions.
API contract flavor detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-api-contract-reviewer` for a specialist review of breaking changes, versioning gaps, and backward-compatibility impact on existing consumers.
Architecture/design concerns visible. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-ask-architecture-strategist` for a structural review (pattern compliance, layer violations, naming consistency).

Looks like a standards/conventions question. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-best-practices-researcher` for synthesizing authoritative docs, community conventions, and idiomatic patterns — with deprecation checks included.
Simplicity concerns visible. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-ask-code-simplicity-reviewer` for a YAGNI/redundancy review.

Prompt looks document-coherence-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-coherence-reviewer` for internal-consistency review: contradictions between sections, terminology drift, broken references, and genuine ambiguity.
Correctness concerns visible. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-ask-correctness-reviewer` for an edge-case / boundary-condition review.
The prompt looks database or migration-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-data-integrity-guardian` for migration safety, constraint validation, transaction boundaries, and data-privacy compliance.

Migration-flavored prompt detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-data-migration-reviewer` for schema-drift detection, backfill safety, deploy-window breaks, enum/mapping correctness, and rollback-plan review.

Prompt looks like a risky data deployment. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-deployment-verification-agent` for Go/No-Go checklists, SQL verification queries, rollback procedures, and post-deploy monitoring plans.
Design-comparison prompt detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-design-implementation-reviewer` for pixel-perfect Figma vs. live UI comparison — spacing, typography, colors & responsive fidelity.
Design refinement prompt detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-design-iterator` for iterative screenshot→analyze→improve cycles that systematically polish UI components to a professional finish.
Design-planning doc detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-design-lens-reviewer` for dimensional review of IA, interaction states, user flows, and AI-slop risk before implementation begins.
Prompt looks plan-feasibility-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-feasibility-reviewer` for architecture conflict, dependency gap, and migration-risk analysis of your technical plan or requirements doc.
Prompt looks design-sync-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-figma-design-sync` for pixel-perfect Figma-to-implementation comparison, CSS/Tailwind fixes, and visual diff documentation.

Docs-research or versioning flavor detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-framework-docs-researcher` for fetching official docs, changelogs, migration guides, and deprecation/sunset notices for frameworks and libraries.

Prompt looks git-archaeology-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-git-history-analyzer` for tracing code evolution, blame/bisect analysis, and contributor mapping to explain why code exists in its current form.
Issue-analysis flavor detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-issue-intelligence-analyst` for surfacing recurring themes, pain patterns, and severity trends across your GitHub issue tracker.
Async/lifecycle-flavored prompt. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-julik-frontend-races-reviewer` for race conditions, stale timers, and lifecycle cleanup in Turbo/Stimulus/async UI code.

Prompt references past learnings or institutional knowledge. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-learnings-researcher` for searching docs/solutions/ frontmatter to surface applicable team knowledge before work starts.

Prompt looks maintainability-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-maintainability-reviewer` for a focused review targeting premature abstraction, dead code, unnecessary indirection, module coupling, and intent-obscuring names.
Pattern/consistency review may help. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-ask-pattern-recognition-specialist` to surface duplication and convention drift.
Performance angle visible. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-ask-performance-oracle` for a structured performance review.
Prompt looks performance-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-performance-reviewer` for expert review of N+1 queries, unbounded memory growth, missing pagination, and hot-path bottlenecks.
Prompt looks PR-thread-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-pr-comment-resolver` for evaluating reviewer threads, implementing fixes, and generating structured reply summaries.
Iterative-review PR context detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-previous-comments-reviewer` for verifying prior reviewer feedback was addressed in the current diff.
Prompt looks product-strategy-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-ask-product-lens-reviewer` for premise challenges, strategic consequences, and goal-alignment review by a senior product leader.
Prompt looks project-standards-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-project-standards-reviewer` for auditing against CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md rules (frontmatter, naming, reference inclusion, portability).
Reliability/failure-mode flavor detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-reliability-reviewer` for a focused review of error handling, retries, timeouts, circuit breakers, and cascading failure paths.
Prompt looks codebase-research-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-repo-research-analyst` for systematic repo research: structure, conventions, patterns, templates, and onboarding insights.

Scope/complexity-flavored planning review. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-scope-guardian-reviewer` for a specialist that challenges unjustified abstractions, scope creep, and over-engineering in plans and requirements docs.

Plan/design-security flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-security-lens-reviewer` for a planning-level security review covering threat models, trust boundaries, auth/authz gaps, and data exposure in specs or design docs.
Diff touches vulnerability-prone code. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-security-reviewer` for attacker-perspective review covering injection, auth/authz bypasses, hardcoded secrets, SSRF, and path traversal.

The prompt looks security-flavored. Consider running `/ce-lite:ce-ask-security-sentinel` (or invoking the wrapper via Skill) for a focused security review by a specialist persona. The wrapper dispatches a sub-agent grounded in OWASP / injection / authn-authz / secrets-handling expertise.
Prompt looks session-history-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-sessions` for synthesizing findings, failed attempts, and key decisions from prior Claude Code / Codex / Cursor sessions on this problem.
Slack-research-flavored prompt. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-slack-researcher` for surfacing team decisions, constraints & undocumented org context from Slack channels.

Spec/flow analysis flavor detected. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-spec-flow-analyzer` for user flow mapping, gap identification, and edge case discovery across specs and feature descriptions before implementation begins.
Swift/iOS-flavored prompt. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-swift-ios-reviewer` for SwiftUI state management, Swift concurrency, Core Data threading, and memory safety review by a senior iOS engineer.
Prompt looks test-quality-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-testing-reviewer` for specialist review of coverage gaps, weak assertions, and brittle implementation-coupled tests.

Prompt looks research-flavored. Consider `/ce-lite:ce-web-researcher` for structured iterative web research — prior art, competitor patterns, market signals, and cross-domain analogies from external sources.
